name: youtube_transcript
title: YouTube Transcript
description: Turn a public YouTube video URL or ID into its full transcript: timestamped caption segments plus a single joined text block, with the language and whether the track is auto-generated.

seo.title: YouTube Transcript Tool for Video Captions and Text
seo.description: Extract a public YouTube video transcript with Better Fetch: timestamped caption segments and a full joined text block from any video URL or ID, ready for summaries, RAG, and analysis.
seo.intro: YouTube Transcript turns any public YouTube video into clean, structured text. It reads the video's caption tracks through the Better Fetch stealth engine, selects the best track for your language, and returns both timestamped segments and one joined transcript string — the ideal input for summarization, search indexing, retrieval-augmented generation, or content repurposing without downloading media.

useCases.title: Summarize long videos
useCases.description: Pull a full transcript and feed it to an LLM to produce summaries, chapter notes, or key takeaways without watching the video.

useCases.title: Search and RAG indexing
useCases.description: Index timestamped segments so agents can retrieve and cite the exact moment a topic is discussed inside a video.

useCases.title: Content repurposing
useCases.description: Convert talks, tutorials, and interviews into text for blog drafts, show notes, and quote extraction.

faqs.question: Does it work on any YouTube video?
faqs.answer: It works on public videos that have captions — either human-written or YouTube's auto-generated track. Videos with captions disabled, or private and age-restricted videos, cannot be transcribed.

faqs.question: Can I choose the transcript language?
faqs.answer: Yes. Pass a language code such as en or es and the tool prefers a matching track, falling back to a manual English track, then any manual track, then auto-generated captions.

faqs.question: What is the difference between the segments and the text field?
faqs.answer: segments are timestamped caption cues with start and duration in seconds, useful for citation and search. text is every segment joined into one string, convenient for summarization prompts.

url.description: Public YouTube video URL (watch, youtu.be, shorts, or embed). Use url or video_id.

video_id.description: 11-character YouTube video id, as an alternative to url.

lang.description: Preferred caption language code, e.g. en or es. Falls back sensibly when unavailable.

max_segments.description: Maximum caption segments to return. The joined text field always covers the returned segments.

lang.description: Language code of the chosen caption track

is_generated.description: True if the track is auto-generated (ASR)

text.description: All segments joined into one transcript string
